mnemonics: Proper Disposal Of Toxic Tech Yields Less Harm (PDOTT-YLH) - Stands for Pollution sources, Disposal methods, Occupational hazards, Toxic components, Treatment processes, Yields from recycling, Landfill risks, Health impacts
keyPoints: E-waste contains hazardous materials like lead, mercury, and cadmium that can leach into soil and water
Informal recycling practices in developing countries cause severe health and environmental pollution
Proper recycling recovers valuable materials and prevents toxic substances from entering the environment
commonMistakes: Assuming throwing e-waste in regular trash is harmless since it's 'just plastic and metal'
Believing that burning e-waste completely eliminates the pollution problem
Thinking all recycling methods are equally effective regardless of safety standards
